pr.title: feat: Phase 1 Provider Profiles Naming Cutover

comments.body: You have reached your Codex usage limits for code reviews. You can see your limits in the [Codex usage dashboard](https://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/usage).

![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The validation requiring `command_template` to be non-empty has been removed. While this may be intentional for "dynamic runtime materialization," it introduces a risk of creating a profile without any command information, which could lead to runtime failures. Please consider adding a validator to `_validate_profile` to ensure that either `command_template` is provided, or another field like `command_behavior` is sufficiently populated to define an executable command.

![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

This test asserts that the `DB_LEASE_PERSISTENCE_PATCH` constant still uses the old `auth-profile-manager` name. In a large-scale renaming effort like this, leaving legacy names creates inconsistency and technical debt. Please rename the constant in `moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/provider_profile_manager.py` to use `provider-profile-manager` and update this test accordingly.

```suggestion
        assert DB_LEASE_PERSISTENCE_PATCH == "provider-profile-manager-db-lease-persistence-v1"
```

## Code Review

This pull request implements Phase 1 of the Provider Profiles migration, renaming the AuthProfileManager subsystem to ProviderProfileManager across the codebase, including Temporal workflows, activities, and Pydantic schemas. Key changes include updating the AgentExecutionRequest to make execution_profile_ref optional and expanding the ManagedRuntimeProfile model. Feedback highlights a potential risk from removing the command_template validator, a weakening of type safety in secret_refs, and several consistency issues such as a legacy string in a persistence constant, absolute file paths in documentation, and typos in test data.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The type of `secret_refs` has been changed from `dict[str, str]` to `dict[str, Any]`. Using `Any` weakens type safety and makes the contract less clear. If `secret_refs` can now hold values other than strings, please use a more specific type, such as a `Union` of expected types or a dedicated Pydantic model, to maintain code clarity and prevent potential runtime type errors.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The `Spec` and `Plan` links use absolute local file paths. This prevents other contributors from using these links. Please update them to be relative paths from the repository root.

```suggestion
**Spec**: `specs/109-provider-profiles-p1/spec.md`
**Plan**: `specs/109-provider-profiles-p1/plan.md`
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

There appears to be a typo in the test data for `profileId`: `"gemini_oprovider_profile"`. This should likely be `"gemini_provider_profile"`. This typo is repeated on lines 108, 131, and 145; please correct it in all occurrences to improve test clarity.

```suggestion
            profileId="gemini_provider_profile",
```

Skipping the entire migration-graph test class means CI will no longer detect broken Alembic graphs, even though this file still asserts specific migration revisions exist. If migrations are intentionally deferred to Phase 2, consider removing/relocating these assertions (or marking them xfail with a tracking issue) rather than skipping, so the test suite continues to provide signal once migrations land.
```suggestion
@pytest.mark.xfail(reason="Migrations land in Phase 2 (tracking: migration graph validation)", strict=True)
```

The mocked `provider_profile.ensure_manager` / `provider_profile.reset_manager` activities still return workflow IDs with the `auth-profile-manager:` prefix. If the cutover is meant to rename workflow IDs to `provider-profile-manager:` (as the Phase 1 plan describes), update these mocks (and any IDs used in the test) to reflect the new canonical prefix; otherwise the test will encode the legacy naming indefinitely.

`ensure_provider_profile_managers_started` still uses `workflow_id = f"auth-profile-manager:{runtime_id}"`. If the goal is a ProviderProfileManager naming cutover (and the Phase 1 plan calls out renaming workflow IDs), consider switching this to `provider-profile-manager:{runtime_id}` (and updating any dependent code/tests). If you intentionally keep the legacy prefix, document it clearly as a compatibility choice.

This test data looks like it has a typo in the profile id: `gemini_oprovider_profile`. If the intent is just a dummy id, consider correcting it to something readable/consistent (e.g. `gemini_provider_profile` or `gemini_oauth_profile`) to avoid confusion when debugging failing tests.

The docstring for `provider_profile_list` still says "List enabled auth profiles". This is user-facing documentation for a renamed activity and should be updated to "provider profiles" to avoid confusion during the cutover.
```suggestion
        """List provider profiles for a runtime family.
```

`import pytest` is reintroduced mid-file even though pytest is already imported at the top. This is redundant and may violate import-order linting (isort/ruff). Remove this extra import and keep imports consolidated at the top of the module.
```suggestion

```

This migration modifies the set of allowed values for the existing `temporalworkflowtype` enum by changing `MoonMind.AuthProfileManager` to `MoonMind.ProviderProfileManager`. If this migration has ever been applied to an environment, editing the historical migration won't update existing DBs and can leave upgrades without an `ALTER TYPE ... ADD VALUE`/replacement path. Prefer a new migration that alters the enum in-place (or a clear reset procedure if this is guaranteed to be pre-release only).
```suggestion
    sa.Column('workflow_type', sa.Enum('MoonMind.Run', 'MoonMind.ManifestIngest', 'MoonMind.AuthProfileManager', name='temporalworkflowtype'), nullable=False),
```

In `_auto_seed_provider_profiles`, the MiniMax seeded profile later sets `secret_refs` to raw env var names (e.g. `"MINIMAX_API_KEY"`). Since the Provider Profiles API validates secret refs using `<backend>://<locator>` (e.g. `env://MINIMAX_API_KEY`), seeding can bypass validation and insert values that downstream secret-ref parsing rejects. Consider normalizing/validating seeded `secret_refs` with the same helper used by the router before committing rows.

## Pull request overview Implements Phase 1 of the Provider Profiles naming/contract cutover by renaming the Temporal “AuthProfileManager” workflow/activity surface area to “ProviderProfileManager”, and updating runtime schemas/tests to support selector-based routing. **Changes:** - Renames Temporal workflow type/name and activity names from `auth_profile.*` / `MoonMind.AuthProfileManager` to `provider_profile.*` / `MoonMind.ProviderProfileManager`. - Updates Pydantic contracts (`AgentExecutionRequest`, `Managed*Profile*`) and exports/tests to reflect the Provider Profile model. - Updates API service enums/startup wiring and related unit/integration tests for the new naming. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 32 out of 32 changed files in this pull request and generated 8 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | tests/unit/workflows/temporal/workflows/test_agent_run_ensure_manager.py | Updates test wording to ProviderProfileManager terminology. | | tests/unit/workflows/temporal/test_temporal_workers.py | Updates registered workflow type expectation to ProviderProfileManager. | | tests/unit/workflows/temporal/test_temporal_worker_runtime.py | Updates worker runtime workflow imports/registrations. | | tests/unit/workflows/temporal/test_provider_profile_manager.py | Renames workflow test suite and activity catalog expectations to provider_profile.*. | | tests/unit/workflows/adapters/test_managed_agent_adapter.py | Updates activity naming in adapter tests to provider_profile_list. | | tests/unit/schemas/test_agent_runtime_models.py | Updates schema tests for renamed ManagedAgentProviderProfile and runtime profile changes. | | tests/unit/api_service/test_provider_profile_enums.py | Adds skip for migration-graph checks (Phase 2), plus import change. | | tests/unit/api_service/test_provider_profile_auto_seed.py | Updates env var name used to skip provider profile seeding. | | tests/unit/api_service/api/routers/test_provider_profiles.py | Renames provider profile CRUD tests to new terminology. | | tests/integration/services/temporal/workflows/test_agent_run.py | Updates activity names and mocked manager workflow name to ProviderProfileManager. | | specs/109-provider-profiles-p1/tasks.md | Adds Phase 1 migration task checklist documentation. | | specs/109-provider-profiles-p1/spec.md | Adds Phase 1 feature spec documentation and acceptance scenarios. | | specs/109-provider-profiles-p1/plan.md | Adds generated implementation plan template doc. | | moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/provider_profile_manager.py | Introduces/renames the manager workflow to ProviderProfileManager and updates activity calls. | | moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/agent_run.py | Switches AgentRun routing to provider_profile.* activities and updated logging strings. | | moonmind/workflows/temporal/workers.py | Updates registered workflow types tuple to ProviderProfileManager. | | moonmind/workflows/temporal/worker_runtime.py | Updates workflow fleet registration to ProviderProfileManager workflow class. | | moonmind/workflows/temporal/service.py | Updates workflow entry mapping to PROVIDER_PROFILE_MANAGER. | | moonmind/workflows/temporal/artifacts.py | Renames TemporalArtifactActivities auth_profile* methods to provider_profile* equivalents. | | moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_runtime.py | Renames activity bindings from auth_profile.* to provider_profile.*. | | moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_catalog.py | Renames catalog activity definitions/family from auth_profile to provider_profile. | | moonmind/workflows/temporal/activities/oauth_session_activities.py | Updates imports to provider profile service/model (but currently has a broken auth-mode import/field usage). | | moonmind/workflows/adapters/managed_agent_adapter.py | Updates docstrings/comments to ProviderProfile naming. | | moonmind/schemas/agent_runtime_models.py | Makes executionProfileRef optional, adds/uses ProfileSelector, renames ManagedAgentProviderProfile, expands ManagedRuntimeProfile. | | moonmind/schemas/__init__.py | Updates exported schema name to ManagedAgentProviderProfile. | | api_service/services/provider_profile_service.py | Updates sync function to use ProviderProfileManagerInput + workflow name. | | api_service/migrations/versions/0b8e4befb8e5_initial_clean_migration.py | Updates TemporalWorkflowType enum values in initial migration. | | api_service/main.py | Updates seed-skip env var and manager startup wiring to ProviderProfileManagerInput. | | api_service/db/models.py | Renames TemporalWorkflowType enum member to PROVIDER_PROFILE_MANAGER. | | api_service/core/sync.py | Updates workflow entry mapping to PROVIDER_PROFILE_MANAGER. | | api_service/api/routers/provider_profiles.py | Switches sync import to provider_profile_service. | | api_service/api/routers/oauth_sessions.py | Switches sync import to provider_profile_service. |
Comments suppressed due to low confidence (8) **api_service/services/provider_profile_service.py:70** * This `start_workflow` call uses `id=workflow_id` where `workflow_id` is still built with the legacy `auth-profile-manager:` prefix in this function. If the intent is a full ProviderProfileManager naming cutover (including workflow IDs), consider switching the workflow_id prefix to `provider-profile-manager:` and updating any dependent callers/tests; otherwise explicitly document that the workflow ID prefix is intentionally unchanged for compatibility. **moonmind/schemas/agent_runtime_models.py:173** * `AgentExecutionRequest` now allows `execution_profile_ref=None`, but the validator does not enforce that some selection criteria is provided (e.g., a non-empty `profileSelector` or an explicit `executionProfileRef`). As written, a request with neither an exact ref nor any selector fields will validate, which can make routing ambiguous and push a hard-to-debug failure downstream. Consider adding a validator rule: if `execution_profile_ref` is None, require at least one meaningful selector field (providerId/tags/runtimeMaterializationMode, etc.). ``` agent_kind: AgentKind = Field(..., alias="agentKind") agent_id: str = Field(..., alias="agentId", min_length=1) execution_profile_ref: str | None = Field(None, alias="executionProfileRef", min_length=1) correlation_id: str = Field(..., alias="correlationId", min_length=1) idempotency_key: str = Field(..., alias="idempotencyKey", min_length=1) instruction_ref: str | None = Field(None, alias="instructionRef") input_refs: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list, alias="inputRefs") expected_output_schema: dict[str, Any] = Field( default_factory=dict, alias="expectedOutputSchema" ) workspace_spec: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, alias="workspaceSpec") parameters: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, alias="parameters") timeout_policy: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, alias="timeoutPolicy") retry_policy: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, alias="retryPolicy") approval_policy: dict[str, Any] = Field( default_factory=dict, alias="approvalPolicy" ) callback_policy: dict[str, Any] = Field( default_factory=dict, alias="callbackPolicy" ) profile_selector: ProfileSelector = Field( default_factory=ProfileSelector, alias="profileSelector" ) @model_validator(mode="after") def _validate_contract(self) -> "AgentExecutionRequest": self.agent_id = _require_non_blank(self.agent_id, field_name="agentId") if self.execution_profile_ref is not None: self.execution_profile_ref = _require_non_blank( self.execution_profile_ref, field_name="executionProfileRef" ) self.correlation_id = _require_non_blank( self.correlation_id, field_name="correlationId" ) self.idempotency_key = _require_non_blank( self.idempotency_key, field_name="idempotencyKey" ) if self.instruction_ref is not None: self.instruction_ref = _require_non_blank( self.instruction_ref, field_name="instructionRef" ) self.input_refs = [ _require_non_blank(item, field_name="inputRefs[]") for item in self.input_refs ] if _contains_sensitive_key(self.parameters): raise ValueError("parameters must not contain raw credential keys") if _contains_sensitive_key(self.workspace_spec): raise ValueError("workspaceSpec must not contain raw credential keys") return self ``` **tests/unit/api_service/api/routers/test_provider_profiles.py:76** * The test name was updated to `test_create_provider_profile`, but the docstring still says "Test creating a new auth profile." Update the docstring to "provider profile" to match the new API terminology. **moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/provider_profile_manager.py:10** * The module docstring still refers to an "auth profile manager" and documents the workflow ID convention as `auth-profile-manager:`, which conflicts with the Phase 1 rename plan (and the new `WORKFLOW_NAME`). Either update the terminology/ID convention to `provider-profile-manager:` everywhere, or explicitly document (and justify) that the workflow_id prefix intentionally remains `auth-profile-manager:` for backward compatibility. **moonmind/workflows/temporal/artifacts.py:2242** * `provider_profile_ensure_manager`/`provider_profile_reset_manager` still start and return workflow IDs using the `auth-profile-manager:` prefix. The Phase 1 plan explicitly calls for renaming workflow IDs to `provider-profile-manager:`; if you keep the old prefix for compatibility, the docstrings and migration plan should reflect that, otherwise update the workflow_id prefix here (and corresponding callers/tests) to complete the naming cutover. ``` """Ensure the ProviderProfileManager workflow is running for *runtime_id*. Starts the singleton ``auth-profile-manager:`` workflow if it is not already running. Handles ``WorkflowAlreadyStartedError`` gracefully so this activity is safe to call repeatedly. """ from temporalio.exceptions import WorkflowAlreadyStartedError from moonmind.workflows.temporal.client import TemporalClientAdapter from moonmind.workflows.temporal.workflows.provider_profile_manager import ( WORKFLOW_NAME as PROVIDER_PROFILE_MANAGER_WF, WORKFLOW_TASK_QUEUE as PROVIDER_PROFILE_MANAGER_QUEUE, ) workflow_id = f"auth-profile-manager:{runtime_id}" adapter = TemporalClientAdapter() ``` **api_service/services/provider_profile_service.py:69** * The `ProviderProfileManagerInput(...)` call is formatted as `ProviderProfileManagerInput(runtime_id=runtime_id,),` (comma inside the constructor call). This is syntactically valid but will likely fail Black formatting checks and is harder to read/maintain. Reformat the call so the constructor args are cleanly formatted. **moonmind/schemas/agent_runtime_models.py:327** * `ManagedRuntimeProfile.command_template` now defaults to an empty list and the previous non-empty validation was removed. Call sites like the launcher/strategies build commands from `profile.command_template`, so accepting an empty template may lead to runtime failures (empty command) that are harder to diagnose than a schema validation error. If empty templates are needed for Phase 2 materialization, consider making the allowance conditional on `runtimeMaterializationMode` (or another explicit flag) and keep the non-empty validation otherwise. ``` auth_mode: str | None = Field(None, alias="authMode") credential_source: str | None = Field(None, alias="credentialSource") runtime_materialization_mode: str | None = Field(None, alias="runtimeMaterializationMode") command_behavior: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, alias="commandBehavior") command_template: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list, alias="commandTemplate") default_model: str | None = Field(None, alias="defaultModel") model_overrides: dict[str, str] = Field(default_factory=dict, alias="modelOverrides") default_effort: str | None = Field(None, alias="defaultEffort") default_timeout_seconds: int = Field(3600, alias="defaultTimeoutSeconds", ge=1) workspace_mode: WorkspaceMode = Field("tempdir", alias="workspaceMode") env_overrides: dict[str, str] = Field(default_factory=dict, alias="envOverrides") env_template: dict[str, str] = Field(default_factory=dict, alias="envTemplate") file_templates: dict[str, str] = Field(default_factory=dict, alias="fileTemplates") home_path_overrides: dict[str, str] = Field(default_factory=dict, alias="homePathOverrides") passthrough_env_keys: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list, alias="passthroughEnvKeys") clear_env_keys: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list, alias="clearEnvKeys") secret_refs: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict, alias="secretRefs") @model_validator(mode="after") def _validate_profile(self) -> "ManagedRuntimeProfile": self.runtime_id = _require_non_blank( self.runtime_id, field_name="runtimeId" ) if _contains_sensitive_key( self.env_overrides, allowed_sensitive_keys=_ALLOWED_MANAGED_LAUNCH_METADATA_KEYS, ): ``` **moonmind/workflows/temporal/activities/oauth_session_activities.py:296** * `oauth_session_register_profile` imports/uses `ManagedAgentAuthMode`, but `api_service.db.models` no longer defines it (the session model uses `ProviderCredentialSource`). This will raise ImportError at runtime and also writes a non-existent `auth_mode` field onto `ManagedAgentProviderProfile`. Update this activity to use the new columns (e.g., set `credential_source`/`runtime_materialization_mode` as appropriate) and remove the `ManagedAgentAuthMode` dependency. ``` async with get_async_session_context() as db: from sqlalchemy.future import select from api_service.db.models import ManagedAgentProviderProfile, ManagedAgentRateLimitPolicy, ManagedAgentAuthMode from api_service.services.provider_profile_service import sync_provider_profile_manager result = await db.execute( select(ManagedAgentOAuthSession).where( ManagedAgentOAuthSession.session_id == session_id ) ) session_obj = result.scalars().first() if not session_obj: raise ValueError(f"Session {session_id} not found") profile_result = await db.execute( select(ManagedAgentProviderProfile).where( ManagedAgentProviderProfile.profile_id == session_obj.profile_id ) ) existing_profile = profile_result.scalars().first() metadata = session_obj.metadata_json or {} policy_str = metadata.get("rate_limit_policy", ManagedAgentRateLimitPolicy.BACKOFF.value) try: policy_enum = ManagedAgentRateLimitPolicy(policy_str) except ValueError: policy_enum = ManagedAgentRateLimitPolicy.BACKOFF profile_data = { "runtime_id": session_obj.runtime_id, "auth_mode": ManagedAgentAuthMode.OAUTH, "volume_ref": session_obj.volume_ref, "volume_mount_path": session_obj.volume_mount_path, ```
